skill.name: ddd-strategic-design
skill.description: Design DDD strategic artifacts including subdomains, bounded contexts, and ubiquitous language for complex business domains.
skill.summary: Map complex business domains into subdomains, bounded contexts, and shared domain language.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography and external command patterns, but the cited evidence is non-executable documentation. The weak cryptography alerts are matches on words such as Design, and the backtick alerts are Markdown formatting, not shell execution.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The cited lines contain skill metadata, headings, tags, or prose using the word Design. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing call, key handling, or encryption workflow appears in these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact cited lines are plain Markdown and YAML text about domain-driven design. I found no evidence of weak cryptographic algorithm usage or related code.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The cited backticks mark an inline file reference and a fenced text example. They are documentation syntax and do not execute Ruby, shell, or any external command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is a Markdown instruction and a fenced text example. There is no script block, command substitution, interpreter invocation, or user input path.

content.value_statement: Complex domains are difficult to split into clear ownership and language boundaries. This skill guides DDD strategic design with subdomain classification, bounded contexts, and glossary artifacts.
actual_capabilities: Classifies business capabilities as core, supporting, or generic subdomains.
Defines bounded contexts around ownership, consistency needs, and domain responsibility.
Builds a ubiquitous language glossary with canonical terms and context-specific meanings.
Creates strategic design artifacts such as context catalogs and boundary decisions.
Connects domain boundaries to likely team ownership and implementation planning.
limitations: It does not produce executable code or tactical DDD implementation patterns.
It depends on accurate stakeholder input and cannot infer business truth alone.
It does not replace workshops with domain experts for high-stakes boundary decisions.
It is not intended for purely infrastructure, UI, or deployment planning tasks.

use_cases.title: Split a Monolith
use_cases.description: Identify bounded contexts and ownership boundaries before extracting services from a large application.

use_cases.title: Align Product Teams
use_cases.description: Map business capabilities to context ownership so teams can plan clearer responsibilities.

use_cases.title: Prepare Discovery Workshops
use_cases.description: Create a starting glossary and subdomain map for sessions with domain experts.

prompt_templates.title: Classify Subdomains
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ddd-strategic-design to classify these capabilities as core, supporting, or generic subdomains: [capabilities]. Explain the reason for each choice.

prompt_templates.title: Define Bounded Contexts
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ddd-strategic-design to propose bounded contexts for this domain: [domain description]. Include responsibilities, owners, and key dependencies.

prompt_templates.title: Build Ubiquitous Language
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ddd-strategic-design to create a ubiquitous language glossary for [domain]. Include canonical terms, definitions, contexts, and ambiguous terms to avoid.

prompt_templates.title: Create Strategic Design Pack
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ddd-strategic-design to produce a strategic design pack for [business domain]. Include subdomains, bounded contexts, glossary, ownership model, and ADR-ready boundary decisions.

output_examples.input: Map a commerce platform with catalog, pricing, checkout, fulfillment, identity, and reporting.
output: A subdomain classification table that marks pricing and checkout as likely core areas.
A bounded context catalog for catalog, checkout, fulfillment, identity, and reporting.
A glossary that separates terms such as order, reservation, payment authorization, and shipment.

output_examples.input: Help decide whether billing and checkout should be one context.
output_examples.output: The skill would compare responsibilities, consistency rules, ownership, upstream dependencies, and downstream consumers before recommending a combined or separate boundary.

output_examples.input: Prepare notes for a domain discovery workshop.
output: A starter list of domain capabilities to validate with stakeholders.
Open questions about ownership, terminology conflicts, and integration points.
Draft boundary decisions that can become ADRs after review.

best_practices: Provide real business capabilities, policies, stakeholders, and pain points as input.
Validate all suggested boundaries with domain experts before implementation.
Record boundary decisions and glossary terms before starting tactical design.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to generate code-level entities before strategic boundaries are clear.
Do not treat suggested subdomain classifications as final without business review.
Do not force service boundaries to match existing technical systems without domain evidence.

faq.question: What does this skill produce?
faq.answer: It produces strategic DDD artifacts such as subdomain classifications, bounded context catalogs, glossaries, and boundary decision notes.

faq.question: Does it generate application code?
faq.answer: No. The skill is focused on strategic design artifacts, not tactical patterns or executable code.

faq.question: Can it replace a domain discovery workshop?
faq.answer: No. It can prepare and structure the work, but domain experts must validate the result.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: When is this skill most useful?
faq.answer: Use it when a business domain needs clearer ownership, language, and service boundaries.

faq.question: What input improves the output?
faq.answer: Provide capabilities, workflows, team ownership, domain terms, integrations, and known business rules.
